scope_note: QA Algebraic Diversity Observer Cert [501]. CLAIM (narrow): The G-function G(b,e)=(b+e)^2+e^2 over QA mod-9 orbits defines algebraically diverse observer channels in the sense of Thornton's Algebraic Diversity framework (arXiv:2604.03634, arXiv:2604.19983). Four checks: (AD_1) G injective on each Cosmos orbit: 24 pairwise-distinct G-values per orbit — integer form of 'Z/24Z is the minimal matched group' (Thornton Replacement Theorem arXiv:2604.03634); (AD_2) G injective on Satellite orbit: 8 pairwise-distinct G-values — Z/8Z minimal matched group; (AD_3) no proper divisor k|24 (k<24) yields G-period-k repetition in any Cosmos orbit, and no proper divisor k|8 (k<8) in Satellite — Z/24Z and Z/8Z are MINIMAL matched groups (Thornton blind matching arXiv:2604.19983); (AD_4) Satellite G-set {45,90,117,153,180,225,261,306} is disjoint from all Cosmos G-sets — Satellite and Cosmos are algebraically diverse non-overlapping observer channels. NOT CLAIMED: DFT computation (float observer), blind group matching algorithm, continuous AD framework (2605.00848), quantum AD (2604.03725), or any continuous-time dynamics. All checks are pure integer arithmetic. Companion: cert [496] E8 Satellite chamber; cert [500] Cosmos G-arithmetic; cert [499] full Pisano partition. Primary sources: Thornton M.A. (2026) arXiv:2604.03634; Thornton M.A. (2026) arXiv:2604.19983; Wildberger N.J. (2005) ISBN 978-0-9757492-0-8. Derived 2026-06-23.
